scenario.prompt: Record that Bob works on Project Phoenix.
Start tracking a new project called Atlas — it's a mobile app rewrite.
Search your memory for anything related to engineers.
Tell me everything you have stored about Alice and Bob.
What do you know about Acme Corp?
Show me your entire memory graph — everything you know.
Forget everything about Bob — remove him from your memory completely.
Alice no longer likes coffee. Remove just that one observation about her, keep the rest.
Alice no longer leads Project Phoenix. Remove that relationship but keep both Alice and the project in memory.

descriptionOverrides.open_nodes: Retrieve the complete information for specific entities by their exact names. Use this when the user asks about known entities by name (e.g., 'What do you know about Alice?'). Use search_nodes instead for fuzzy or keyword searches.
descriptionOverrides.search_nodes: Search for nodes using a fuzzy keyword or text query. Use this when the user provides partial descriptions or keywords rather than exact names. Use open_nodes instead when you already know the exact entity names.
descriptionOverrides.create_entities: Create new entities in the knowledge graph. Use this when the user asks you to remember, record, or store information about new subjects (e.g., 'Carol is a designer'). Call this directly without searching first. Use add_observations instead to add facts to existing entities.
descriptionOverrides.create_relations: Create relations between entities in the knowledge graph. Use this when the user asks to record a connection between two entities (e.g., 'Bob works on Project Phoenix'). Call this directly without first calling create_entities. Relations should be in active voice.
descriptionOverrides.read_graph: Read the entire knowledge graph. Use this only when the user explicitly asks for everything or the whole graph. Use open_nodes instead to look up specific entities by name.
